competencies.name: Proactivity / Initiative
questions: Tell me about a time you identified a problem or opportunity that nobody asked you to deal with. What did you do?
Describe a situation where you went beyond what was expected of you. What drove you to do that?
Tell me about a time you spotted something that wasn't working and decided to do something about it, even without being asked.
When have you created something — a process, a project, an idea — from scratch because you saw a gap?
Describe a time you took ownership of something that was technically outside your responsibility. Why did you step up?
competencies.followup_goal: Find out if they actually drove it forward or just noticed it. Look for ownership of the outcome, not just the observation.

competencies.name: Learning Mindset / Hunger to Grow
questions: What's something you've deliberately taught yourself in the past year — not for an exam, but because you wanted to? How did you go about it?
Tell me about a time you actively sought out feedback or a new perspective, even when you didn't have to. What did you do with it?
Describe a moment where you realized you had a significant gap in your knowledge or skills. How did you respond?
What's the last thing you pursued purely out of curiosity — a book, a course, a side project — and what did you get out of it?
Tell me about a time you changed your mind about something important because of something you learned. What shifted?
competencies.followup_goal: Distinguish genuine curiosity from performance of curiosity. Look for self-direction, specificity, and whether they can describe their own learning process.

competencies.name: Adaptability
questions: Describe a situation where your plan didn't work and you had to change direction under pressure. What happened?
Tell me about a time when the rules of the game changed mid-way through a project or task. How did you respond?
Describe a moment where you had to abandon work you'd already done and start fresh. How did you handle that?
Tell me about a time you got completely unexpected news that changed your priorities. What did you do?
When have you had to quickly shift between very different tasks or contexts? How did you manage?
competencies.followup_goal: Find out whether they adapted thoughtfully or just reacted. Look for awareness of why the plan failed and deliberate choice of the new direction.

competencies.name: Collaboration / Team Orientation
questions: Tell me about a time you had to work closely with someone whose working style or opinion was very different from yours. How did you handle it?
Describe a situation where your team was struggling to work together. What did you do?
Tell me about a time you had to put the team's goal ahead of your own preference or idea. How did you approach that?
When have you had to build trust or a working relationship quickly with people you didn't know? What did you do?
Describe a time when you helped someone else on your team succeed — not because it was your job, but because you saw they needed it.
competencies.followup_goal: Look for genuine team orientation, not just the ability to tolerate others. Find out if they adapt their behavior for the team, or just describe cooperation as a value.

competencies.name: Self-Reflection / Learning from Feedback
questions: Tell me about a piece of feedback you received that was hard to hear. What did you do with it?
Describe a time when you realized your own assumptions were wrong. What did you do when you figured that out?
Tell me about a failure or near-failure. What happened, and what did you change afterward?
When have you actively sought out criticism on your own work? How did you use it?
Tell me about a time you had to admit you'd made a mistake — to someone else or to yourself. What did that look like?
competencies.followup_goal: Distinguish candidates who genuinely integrate feedback from those who just say they do. Look for specificity — what changed in their actual behavior.
